content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "00_inbox/_README.md", "name": "_README.md", "extension": ".md", "mime_type": "text/markdown", "size_bytes": 316, "zone": "00_inbox", "sample_text": "# 00_inbox\n\nUse this zone for new or ambiguous material only.\n\nSubfolders:\n\n- `downloads`: raw intake from browsers, apps, and exports.\n- `imports`: files copied from other systems or devices.\n- `unsorted`: ambiguous items awaiting classification.\n\nNothing should stay here indefinitely. Triage this zone regularly."}]

thoughts.subject: Defining the File Classifier
thoughts.description: I'm currently focused on defining the structure of the file classification output. My initial thought is a JSON array of objects. Each object will represent a file and will need fields for `rel_path`, `title`, `content_kind`, and `domain`. I am refining the specific values and logic that will populate these fields, based on the provided file attributes.

thoughts.subject: Evaluating Field Mappings
thoughts.description: I'm now iterating on the field mappings based on the README file. The `domain` field presents a choice: "infra" or "ai". I lean towards "infra" because the text describes a file system structure. I'm also planning to include "lifecycle" which will likely be "temporary" or "active", based on the zone and file nature. Furthermore, I will create a summary extracting the most critical parts from `sample_text`.

thoughts.subject: Finalizing the Classification
thoughts.description: I'm now satisfied with the file classification. My final adjustments involved refining the `domain` and `tags` fields. I've chosen `"infra"` for the domain, because it best represents the content of the file. I have adjusted the tags. I'm ready to move on.

thoughts.subject: Defining the JSON Output
thoughts.description: I'm now generating the JSON output structure. I've successfully mapped the fields for `rel_path`, `title`, and `content_kind`. Refining the  `domain` and `tags` fields is underway, and I am also working on creating an improved one-sentence `summary`, along with defining the `entities` and `relations` objects. Further, I am considering the `canonical_priority` and `recommended_zone`.
